summary:
Early discharge compromises timely blood sample collection necessary for accurate newborn metabolic screening, as most tests require specimens obtained within 24-72 hours after birth. Nurse home visits support ongoing care but cannot compensate for lost time during this crucial period, confirming that such discharges reduce overall screening effectiveness. 

Note: The summary is strictly limited to two sentences focused solely on clinically significant aspects related to screen efficacy and timing. No mention of non-clinical factors like cost-effectiveness or parental satisfaction was included.
